Response: KU Leuven / PMA Division, Belgium; founded by Herman Bruyninckx (KU Leuven, December 2000); initial implementation by Peter Soetens (PhD student KU Leuven); copyright shared by 20+ contributors internationally

Metric: Software purpose (string)
Response: Open-source C++ framework for real-time robot control; major components: Real-Time Toolkit (RTT) for component-based hard real-time software; Kinematics and Dynamics Library (KDL) for serial chain kinematics; Bayesian Filtering Library (BFL); Orocos Component Library (OCL)

Metric: Number of developers (all developers that have contributed at least one commit to the project) (use repo commit logs) (number)
Response: 20+ (copyright shared by 20+ contributors; contributions from KU Leuven

Response: unclear (historically: KU Leuven institutional support, EU and Belgian national research grants; current status unclear)

Metric: Initial release date? (date)
Response: December 2000 (project conceived); first OROCOS major release September 2003; KDL originated circa 2005

Metric: Last commit date? (date)
Response: 2024-06 (orocos_kinematics_dynamics KDL repository; KDL maintained; RTT less active)

column4: OROCOS is one of the oldest open-source real-time robot control frameworks. KDL became the standard kinematic chain library in ROS 1. ROS robot_state_publisher, trac-ik, and many ROS IK plugins build on KDL. LGPL/GPL runtime exception license was chosen to allow C++ template use without GPL contamination.
